drafts/features/2026-04-22/anthropic-fea.top_viral_mechanic: Data-literate traders can spot and call out algos caught in lucky streaks, creating native credibility plays on r/algotrading by showing they read the fine print.
drafts/features/2026-04-22/anthropic-fea.biggest_weakness: No incentive to share the finding—spotting a divergence is personal alpha, not social proof, so users hoard rather than broadcast.

drafts/features/2026-04-22/anthropic-fea.top_viral_mechanic: Transparency of failure reasons (algo moved to noise: 40% win rate) lets users share audit logs as proof of rigorous methodology, exploiting the core unfair advantage of publishing failures.
drafts/features/2026-04-22/anthropic-fea.biggest_weakness: No invitation mechanic or network effect—users audit algos alone; sharing a bucket transition log is interesting only to existing users, not a recruitment tool.

drafts/features/2026-04-22/anthropic-fea.top_viral_mechanic: Users can publicly claim they spotted a signal-type pattern before others (e.g., 'I saw weather algos were noise 3 months ago'), turning data analysis into social proof.
drafts/features/2026-04-22/anthropic-fea.biggest_weakness: User-only tagging with zero sharing or public leaderboard means patterns discovered stay locked in private dashboards—no built-in mechanism to broadcast findings or create FOMO.

drafts/features/2026-04-22/anthropic-fea.top_viral_mechanic: Data exports with explicit 'no survivorship bias' headers let skeptical traders prove to peers that stockarithm is genuinely transparent—the anti-hype flex in a hype category.
drafts/features/2026-04-22/anthropic-fea.biggest_weakness: Zero network effects or invitation mechanics; a user exporting data doesn't pull anyone else into the product, so growth is entirely dependent on individual conversion.

drafts/features/2026-04-23/anthropic-fea.top_viral_mechanic: Lets skeptical traders spot and share algos that are visibly recovering from failure — pure narrative ammunition for the 'receipts guy' archetype who loves proving others wrong with data.
drafts/features/2026-04-23/anthropic-fea.biggest_weakness: No incentive loop or sharing mechanic; a user finds a hot divergence but has zero reason to tell anyone else — it's pure consumption, not co-creation or social proof.

drafts/features/2026-04-23/anthropic-fea.top_viral_mechanic: Inspection-level transparency (signal conditions, SPY comparison) lets alt-data nerds prove signal legitimacy publicly in Reddit debates without screenshots—'Biscotti fired on [condition] while SPY did [this]' is shareable proof.
drafts/features/2026-04-23/anthropic-fea.biggest_weakness: Read-only audit with no export or share button means users must manually transcribe findings to social; feature lives inside the product and doesn't naturally become a social artifact or conversation starter.

drafts/features/2026-04-23/anthropic-fea.top_viral_mechanic: Operator thesis becomes a public proof artifact—readers can screenshot/share the explicit failure logic and prediction criteria, which is rare and credible in fintech.
drafts/features/2026-04-23/anthropic-fea.biggest_weakness: No incentive for users to *invite others* to see theses; it's consumption-only and doesn't leverage the two-sided market (winners/losers) as a shared discovery mechanism.

drafts/features/2026-04-23/anthropic-fea.top_viral_mechanic: Shows classification volatility as proof of rigor—users can cite 'Biscotti bounced 4x in 60 days' to warn peers, leveraging the failures-published credibility angle.
drafts/features/2026-04-23/anthropic-fea.biggest_weakness: Purely analytical; no social sharing mechanic, invitation loop, or reason for users to expose the feature to their network—it's internal due diligence, not social currency.

drafts/features/2026-04-23/openai-featur.top_viral_mechanic: Loss flags directly amplify stockarithm's core unfair advantage (transparency) and give skeptical traders proof-of-honesty social currency when they cite it to peers.
drafts/features/2026-04-23/openai-featur.biggest_weakness: Loss flags are table stakes for credibility, not shareable content—users don't screenshot loss data to flex or tell friends about, so invitation loop and public evidence are near zero.

drafts/features/2026-04-23/openai-featur.top_viral_mechanic: Bucket classification directly leverages stockarithm's unique failures-published transparency—users can visibly filter ALPHA signals and share their curation, turning data triage into credible signal curation.
drafts/features/2026-04-23/openai-featur.biggest_weakness: Feature is UI/UX optimization with zero incentive loop—users don't gain social currency for using it, can't easily share their bucket filters, and there's no reason others need to join to unlock value.

drafts/features/2026-04-23/openai-featur.top_viral_mechanic: Lets expert traders spot anomalies others miss and share 'Biscotti found a weird pattern' moments that prove their edge.
drafts/features/2026-04-23/openai-featur.biggest_weakness: No built-in sharing mechanism or leaderboard footprint—divergence discovery stays private unless user manually screenshots and posts.

drafts/features/2026-04-23/openai-featur.top_viral_mechanic: Foregrounds losses publicly with red/green color coding, exploiting stockarithm's radical transparency angle to let power users prove their due diligence to skeptical communities.
drafts/features/2026-04-23/openai-featur.biggest_weakness: Zero network effects or invitation mechanics—a Receipts Guy auditing trades alone doesn't create incentive or reason to share with others, making it purely consumptive.

drafts/features/2026-04-24/anthropic-fea.top_viral_mechanic: Transparency-first leaderboard column directly proves stockarithm publishes failures, turning the core unfair advantage into a visible, shareable proof point that skeptical traders can screenshot and cite.
drafts/features/2026-04-24/anthropic-fea.biggest_weakness: Zero network effects or invitation mechanics—users don't need to recruit others to see drawdowns, and the feature doesn't create a reason to share or collaborate with peers.

drafts/features/2026-04-24/anthropic-fea.top_viral_mechanic: Operator's honest classification (ALPHA/NOISE/UNRESOLVED/DEAD) with visible override notes lets users cite the lab's own skepticism as proof they're not following hype—high social currency in anti-hype communities.
drafts/features/2026-04-24/anthropic-fea.biggest_weakness: No incentive for users to invite others or share their bucket discoveries; filtering and categorization are personal utility, not network effects or public artifacts.

scores.public_evidence_note: sparkline is internal leaderboard UI, not shareable artifact

drafts/features/2026-04-24/anthropic-fea.top_viral_mechanic: Exposes algo fade-outs and hot streaks in a way that rewards skeptical users who distrust all-time rankings—fits the transparency ethos perfectly.
drafts/features/2026-04-24/anthropic-fea.biggest_weakness: Zero social sharing mechanism; a user discovering a hot streak has no reason to tell anyone else, and the sparkline itself is not a portable proof artifact.

reasoning.proof_of_work: Sparkline helps users *spot* divergence but doesn't let them *prove* they found it first or that their thesis was right. No badge, no shareable insight, no 'I called this fade' moment.
reasoning.invitation_loop: No incentive for users to invite others. The feature works identically whether you're alone or in a crowd.
reasoning.public_evidence: Sparkline lives inside the leaderboard. It's not a tweet, a screenshot worth sharing, or a public claim. Internal UI has zero viral surface.
reasoning.cooperative_gain: More users don't make the sparkline better. It's a read-only visualization of historical rank data.
reasoning.build_complexity: Low friction: add a column, compute rolling rank windows, render sparkline. ~2–3 days for a solid implementation. Data already exists.
reasoning.stockarithm_fit: High fit: directly exploits the 'failures published' angle and the Receipts Guy persona. Sparkline is *only* valuable because stockarithm publishes both winners and losers. Generic fintech dashboards don't need this.

drafts/features/2026-04-24/anthropic-fea.recommendation: This is a solid product feature that deepens engagement for power users (Receipts Guy, Signal Hunters) but has no viral hook. Build it *after* launch as a polish feature. Pre-launch, prioritize features with built-in sharing or network effects (e.g., 'Algo Twins' that shows which signals agree/disagree, or a 'Fade Alert' that users can subscribe to and share when an algo breaks its streak).

drafts/features/2026-04-24/anthropic-fea.top_viral_mechanic: Receipts Guys can download auditable trade logs and publicly verify the lab isn't gaming numbers—this is proof-of-work currency in a skeptical community that despises hidden losses.
drafts/features/2026-04-24/anthropic-fea.biggest_weakness: Zero network effects or invitation loops; one user downloading a CSV doesn't pull in others, and there's no natural sharing mechanism beyond 'I checked the receipts myself.'

drafts/features/2026-04-24/openai-featur.top_viral_mechanic: Visualizing failures with equal weight to wins directly exploits stockarithm's transparency unfair advantage and lets data-literate users spot contrarian signals worth discussing in r/algotrading.
drafts/features/2026-04-24/openai-featur.biggest_weakness: No invitation loop or network effect—users consume the insight alone; decay curves don't naturally prompt sharing or require others to join for value unlock.

drafts/features/2026-04-24/openai-featur.top_viral_mechanic: Failure leaderboard with zero euphemisms directly contradicts fintech norms, making users who share it look intellectually honest to skeptical communities like r/algotrading.
drafts/features/2026-04-24/openai-featur.biggest_weakness: No invitation loop or network effect—users consume failure data but have zero reason to recruit others; it's a spectator feature, not a participation multiplier.

drafts/features/2026-04-24/openai-featur.top_viral_mechanic: Torn-paper lab-notebook aesthetic + unfiltered failure language creates authentic credibility that skeptical traders want to screenshot and share as proof of non-hype.
drafts/features/2026-04-24/openai-featur.biggest_weakness: Hover overlay is a passive information tool with zero incentive for users to invite others or create shareable moments—it solves friction but doesn't create word-of-mouth.

drafts/features/2026-04-24/openai-featur.top_viral_mechanic: Transparent failure logging with zero obfuscation directly contradicts fintech norms and gives data-literate traders credibility ammunition to share ('look how honest this lab is').
drafts/features/2026-04-24/openai-featur.biggest_weakness: No built-in sharing mechanism, invitation loop, or network effect—users consume the timeline privately; it doesn't naturally propagate to their networks or require others to join.
